top_weaknesses.issue: Multiple unsupported certainty-claims, especially 'The 2025 to 2026 deployment data confirms it' with no link or source — a credibility gap that gets flagged across personas
top_weaknesses.fix: Either cite a specific paper/dataset/company evidence or soften to 'early evidence suggests'

top_weaknesses.issue: Heavy metaphor/template diction: 'walks into the office', 'stripped to its skeleton', 'fits this template too cleanly to ignore', 'inequality machine', 'escape hatch nobody is funding', 'hollowing out the middle' — reads as thinkpiece template
top_weaknesses.fix: Replace each metaphor section title with a plainer promise (e.g., 'How so-so automation works (3 steps)', 'How AI shifts income from labor to capital'); cut 'the chapter has a name' flourish

top_weaknesses.issue: Thesis is delayed — first paragraph leads with stats and only paragraph 2 names 'so-so automation'; the snippet zone could carry the definition + stake in one breath
top_weaknesses.fix: Move 'GenAI is Acemoglu's so-so automation' into sentence 1-2 of the lede

top_weaknesses.issue: One paragraph clocks ~82 words (the substitution-play paragraph), borderline wall on mobile
top_weaknesses.fix: Split at 'A senior litigator with an AI research assistant...'

consensus_summary: Sharp economic frame and coherent H2 arc, but the prose leans into thinkpiece metaphors and asserts fresh empirical claims without sources. De-slop the metaphors, source the 2025-26 claim, and move the thesis into the lede.
